capacity.description: Specifies the capacity. When sku is Premium, capacity can be 1, 2, 4, 8 or 16. When sku is Basic or Standard, capacity can be 0 only.

identity_id.description: The ID of the User Assigned Identity that has access to the key. Required when key_vault_key_id is set.

user_managed_identities.description: List of user managed identities to assign to the namespace

ip_rules.description: One or more IP Addresses, or CIDR Blocks which should be able to access the ServiceBus Namespace.

key_vault_key_id.description: The dataplane ID of the Key Vault Key which should be used to Encrypt the data in this ServiceBus Namespace. Note: Once customer-managed key encryption has been enabled, it cannot be disabled.

local_auth_enabled.description: Whether or not SAS authentication is enabled for the Service Bus namespace. Defaults to true.

minimum_tls_version.description: The minimum supported TLS version for this Service Bus Namespace. Valid values are: 1.0, 1.1 and 1.2. The current default minimum TLS version is 1.2.

name.description: Specifies the name of the ServiceBus Namespace resource . Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

network_rule_set_default_action.description: Specifies the default action for the Network Rule Set. If allowed network ruleset will be created on resource.

allowed_subnet_ids.description: List of subnet resource ids to allow access to the namespace. `public_network_access_enabled` must be set to true to allow traffic from these subnets.

premium_messaging_partitions.description: Specifies the number messaging partitions. Only valid when sku is Premium and the minimum number is 1. Possible values include 0, 1, 2, and 4. Defaults to 0 for Standard, Basic namespace. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

public_network_access_enabled.description: Enable public network access. Disabling it will allow database access only via private endpoints.

sku.description: Defines which tier to use. Options are Basic, Standard or Premium. Please note that setting this field to Premium will force the creation of a new resource.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

trusted_services_allowed.description: Are Azure Services that are known and trusted for this resource type are allowed to bypass firewall configuration? See Trusted Microsoft Services - https://github.com/MicrosoftDocs/azure-docs/blob/main/articles/service-bus-messaging/includes/service-bus-trusted-services.md

zone_redundant.description: Whether or not this resource is zone redundant. sku needs to be Premium. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

resource_group_name.description: Name of the resource group that this resource will be created in.

location.description: Location that this resource will be created in. (e.g. eastus2, southeastasia)
